channel.title: Twitter - @elonmusk
channel.description: 自动生成的Twitter RSS - 最后更新: 2026-04-25 14:11 UTC | 来源实例: https://xcancel.com

item.title: @elonmusk: It’s interesting that the Southern Poverty Law Centre gave money to the KKK. What I also find intere...
item.description: It’s interesting that the Southern Poverty Law Centre gave money to the KKK. What I also find interesting is that the KKK accepted it. All collectivists together, eh?

item.title: @elonmusk: The fact that I wasn’t funded by the SPLC proves I’m not a Nazi
item.description: The fact that I wasn’t funded by the SPLC proves I’m not a Nazi

item.title: @elonmusk: But without racism, who will fund the Southern Poverty Law Center (and all the other thousands of fa...
item.description: But without racism, who will fund the Southern Poverty Law Center (and all the other thousands of far-left non-profits)?

item.title: @elonmusk: Incentives explain outcomes. Actually eliminating hatred would end the existence of “anti-hate” grou...
item.description: Incentives explain outcomes. Actually eliminating hatred would end the existence of “anti-hate” groups, so groups like SPLC amplified hatred instead to get more donations.

item.title: @elonmusk: SPLC got better returns than Nancy Pelosi and inverse Cramer combined!
item.description: SPLC got better returns than Nancy Pelosi and inverse Cramer combined!

item.title: @elonmusk: I recently awarded Britain the coveted title of Stage 5 Suicidal Empath. A just award.
item.description: I recently awarded Britain the coveted title of Stage 5 Suicidal Empath. A just award.

item.title: @elonmusk: We need Nuremberg trials for the monsters that enabled this.
item.description: We need Nuremberg trials for the monsters that enabled this.

item.title: @elonmusk: grok imagine is on another level now. the new model is unreal. go try it.
item.description: grok imagine is on another level now. the new model is unreal. go try it.

item.title: @elonmusk: Whoah, self-driving cars compete with airlines. I never considered that till now.
item.description: Whoah, self-driving cars compete with airlines. I never considered that till now.

item.title: @elonmusk: You can't trust Chat GPT. It is funding partisan left-wing groups while pretending to be 'unbiased.'
item.description: You can't trust Chat GPT. It is funding partisan left-wing groups while pretending to be 'unbiased.'

item.title: @elonmusk: The Japanese railway privatization of 1987 stands as one of the most devastating defeats ever dealt ...
item.description: The Japanese railway privatization of 1987 stands as one of the most devastating defeats ever dealt to statist transportation mythology. The government split the bloated Japan National Railways into seven regional companies, sold them off, and watched private ownership transform a bankruptcy-bound disaster into the world's most efficient rail system. JNR hemorrhaged money for decades before privatization. By 1987, the state railway carried debt equivalent to $200 billion in today's money while delivering mediocre service plagued by strikes and inefficiency. Politicians treated it as a jobs program rather than a transportation service. The predictable result: chronic losses, deteriorating infrastructure, and customer service that reflected government monopoly arrogance. Private ownership changed everything overnight. The new JR companies slashed operating costs by 40% within five years while dramatically improving service quality. JR East alone now generates annual profits exceeding $3 billion. These companies invest billions in cutting-edge technology, maintain punctuality rates above 99%, and operate the world's most advanced high-speed rail networks. They achieved this without a single yen of operational subsidies. The transformation reveals a core dynamic of transportation infrastructure: private companies must satisfy customers to survive, while government monopolies need only satisfy politicians. JR companies diversified into real estate, retail, and hospitality around their stations, creating integrated profit centers that cross-subsidize rail operations. Government railways never innovate this way because bureaucrats face no market pressure to generate returns. Meanwhile, Amtrak burns through $2 billion in annual subsidies while delivering third-world service across most routes, and European state railways require massive taxpayer bailouts every few years to stay solvent.

item.title: @elonmusk: The Tesla "T" logo comes from Nikola Tesla's original electric motor patent btw Pretty cool.
item.description: The Tesla "T" logo comes from Nikola Tesla's original electric motor patent btw Pretty cool.

item.title: @elonmusk: Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 set the bar. #1 spot. A strong start. 67.3% on the board… others are way b...
item.description: Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 set the bar. #1 spot. A strong start. 67.3% on the board… others are way behind. Not demos. Real calls getting handled. Sales, support, actual outcomes. Used by@Starlink. That says something.@xAI@Grok

item.title: @elonmusk: New Grok Imagine model just dropped with much better lip sync & sound. Nothing in this video is real...
item.description: New Grok Imagine model just dropped with much better lip sync & sound. Nothing in this video is real.
